completion_definition.node_complete_means: Every behavior listed in completes_behaviors is implemented, asserted by executable behavior proof, and demonstrated through the shaped spec walkthrough before the node can be marked complete.
completion_definition.completed_behavior_rule: A roadmap node does not complete a behavior through implementation alone. Completion requires passing behavior-linked proof with a positive witness, a meaningful falsification witness where applicable, and demo or walk evidence showing the behavior is real through the observable surface named by the behavior.
completion_definition.bootstrap_assertion_mode: Before the native behavior-proof subsystem exists, assertion may be satisfied by repo BDD proof and the spec walkthrough record. After native behavior proof exists, assertion must also be visible through behavior-proof and assessment read models.
not_allowed: Marking a node complete while any completed behavior remains only implemented, unproven, or undocumented by the spec walkthrough
Using supports_behaviors as a substitute for completed and asserted behavior
Counting implementation-detail unit tests as behavior assertion unless they are part of behavior-linked executable proof

milestones.title: Rebuild the control-plane kernel
milestones.goal: Replace file-first and direct-service paths with typed event canon, shared contracts, and coherent public surfaces

milestones.title: Make planning native
milestones.goal: Turn product, behavior, architecture, assessment, roadmap, and decision memory into first-party typed planning state

milestones.title: Shape executable specs
milestones.goal: Convert accepted roadmap nodes and intake signals into spec-sized work with readiness, quality, and dependency rules

milestones.title: Run the orchestration loop
milestones.goal: Execute active specs through loop state, blockers, notifications, team coordination, and candidate implementations

milestones.title: Isolate runtime execution
milestones.goal: Run assignments through scoped workers, durable queues, harness adapters, target placement, and redacted results

milestones.title: Prove and review delivered behavior
milestones.goal: Require behavior proof, quality controls, walks, PR validation, merge handoff, and release learning

milestones.title: Govern access and configuration
milestones.goal: Add the identity, policy, approvals, configuration, secret, and credential model needed for safe solo and team use

milestones.title: Integrate providers and clients
milestones.goal: Connect provider integrations, external trackers, webhooks, API clients, and source-control status through adapter-backed contracts

milestones.title: Expose observation and operations
milestones.goal: Provide provenance-aware progress, quality, risk, health, reporting, backup, restore, incident, and audit views

milestones.title: Package Tanren for self-hosting
milestones.goal: Deliver the Compose-first stack, repository assets, standards profiles, installer drift handling, upgrade, and uninstall flows

nodes.title: Build canonical event log and read-model substrate

expected_evidence.description: Positive and falsification scenarios cover idempotent mutations, validation errors, and read-model observation through the event-backed command path

nodes.scope: Create the typed event envelope, append service, idempotency records, projection checkpoints, read-model freshness metadata, and shared validation error categories.
nodes.rationale: The accepted architecture makes the event log primary canon. The rewrite should start here so old file-first paths become salvageable inputs rather than architectural constraints.
risks: Too much subsystem semantics may leak into the generic state layer
shape_notes: Keep this as a narrow kernel; subsystem-specific events should arrive in later nodes.

nodes.title: Stand up shared HTTP interface contracts

expected_evidence.description: Cross-interface scenarios assert coherent state, consistent validation, schema negotiation, backpressure reporting, and safe client replay

nodes.scope: Define API command, query, subscription, cursor, freshness, error, idempotency, versioning, and generated-client contracts for web, CLI, TUI, API, and MCP surfaces.
nodes.rationale: The implementation assessment identifies the empty API and direct-service interface paths as the highest-impact architecture divergence.
risks: Contract breadth can outpace implemented behavior
shape_notes: Prefer thin vertical slices through one shared application-service path over broad route scaffolding.

nodes.title: Bootstrap identity, account, organization, and project scope

expected_evidence.description: First-run scenarios prove setup, deployment posture choice, identity/source-control selection, project creation/connection, project switching, and ready first project outcomes

nodes.scope: Implement first-run account, organization, project, active scope, project connection, project creation, project disconnection, and join-existing-project flows through the HTTP control plane.
nodes.rationale: Solo and team use share one model, so the rewrite needs real scope and identity before planning or execution state is claimed.
risks: Bootstrap ergonomics can hide policy shortcuts that later conflict with team use
shape_notes: Treat local solo setup as a small self-hosted install, not a separate no-auth architecture.

nodes.title: Provide first-party surface shells over the shared contract

expected_evidence.description: Interface scenarios show project overview and changed-since views from API-backed read models with provenance links

nodes.scope: Create thin CLI, MCP, TUI, API, and web entry surfaces that authenticate, call shared contracts, render project overview state, and expose provenance without local mutation bypasses.
nodes.rationale: This makes interface divergence visible early while postponing specialized workflows until their subsystem state exists.
risks: Surface shells may be mistaken for complete workflows
shape_notes: Unsupported actions should return explicit contract responses until later nodes implement them.

nodes.title: Implement native product brief and decision memory

expected_evidence.description: Planning scenarios prove project brief creation, target users, non-goals, constraints, success signals, imported context, assumptions, stale assumptions, and rejected alternatives

nodes.scope: Replace temporary product markdown editing with event-backed product brief, imported context, decision, assumption, preference, and conflict-resolution records plus repo projections.
nodes.rationale: Product intent is the first durable planning layer and must not remain an ad hoc document path during the architecture revision.
risks: Overfitting the model to Tanren's own product vocabulary
shape_notes: Keep project-defined vocabulary open while enforcing lifecycle and projection ownership.

nodes.title: Implement native behavior catalog and coverage state

expected_evidence.description: Behavior catalog scenarios prove accepted behavior maintenance, coverage visibility, bug classification against behavior status, and catalog coherence review

nodes.scope: Add typed behavior records, lifecycle transitions, verification-status projections, behavior coverage summaries, bug-report classification, coherence review, and generated behavior-file projections.
nodes.rationale: Every executable roadmap node and proof record depends on stable accepted behavior contracts.
risks: Verification state may be accidentally stored as behavior acceptance
shape_notes: Keep product status and verification/proof status as separate records.

nodes.title: Implement architecture records and tradeoff review

expected_evidence.description: Architecture scenarios prove architecture record maintenance, tradeoff review, accepted projection generation, and rejected alternative preservation

nodes.scope: Create architecture record events, accepted/draft/proposed lifecycle state, tradeoff review, projection generation, and links to product and behavior context.
nodes.rationale: The mass revision needs architecture as durable planning state rather than a loose set of markdown files.
risks: Architecture review can become too heavyweight for small projects
shape_notes: Support concise records first; subsystem detail can grow through accepted changes.

nodes.title: Implement repository understanding and implementation assessment

expected_evidence.description: Assessment scenarios prove repository analysis, command detection, architecture/risk summary, initial configuration proposals, behavior assessment, and uncertainty review

nodes.scope: Turn current static readiness into typed assessment runs, behavior classifications, uncertainty review, source-signal references, and repo-understanding projections.
nodes.rationale: Existing code should be mined for useful primitives, but assessment must report evidence and uncertainty instead of letting current structure dictate the target system.
risks: Static analysis can overclaim behavior support
shape_notes: Classify the old implementation conservatively and preserve source references for salvage decisions.

nodes.title: Implement roadmap DAG and replanning

expected_evidence.description: Roadmap scenarios prove graph visibility, blocked/next-work explanations, replan changes, graph revision comparisons, approval-gated plans, source-signal links, sequencing, deferral, and behavior coverage

nodes.scope: Create event-backed roadmap revisions, node dependencies, behavior coverage, prioritization comparisons, generated-plan approvals, replan diffs, and roadmap projections.
nodes.rationale: A dependency-aware graph is the planning source of truth for the rewrite and for future execution.
risks: Grouping too many behaviors into one roadmap node can hide proof obligations
shape_notes: Require each executable node to cite accepted behavior and expected proof before it can shape into a spec.

nodes.title: Implement planning proposal, revision recovery, and controlled undo

expected_evidence.description: Planning-change scenarios prove proposal, review, revision restore, follow-up revert, and bad-decision recovery without deleting history

nodes.scope: Add proposal records, accepted/rejected/deferred review, revision restore, landed-work revert through follow-up, and recovery from bad planning decisions.
nodes.rationale: A rewrite roadmap will change as evidence arrives; revisions need history-preserving mutation rather than direct artifact edits.
risks: Restore semantics can be confused with destructive rollback
shape_notes: Corrections must append new events and regenerate projections.

nodes.title: Implement draft spec intake and candidate creation

expected_evidence.description: Intake scenarios prove roadmap-to-spec conversion plus manual, ticket, feedback, meeting-note, and audit-finding candidate creation

nodes.scope: Create draft spec candidate records from roadmap nodes, manual input, external tickets, customer feedback, meeting notes, and audit findings.
nodes.rationale: Ideas and source signals should enter delivery through typed candidates rather than bypassing planning.
risks: Intake can create too many unprioritized candidates
shape_notes: Candidate creation does not make work ready; readiness belongs to shape-spec.

nodes.title: Implement shape-spec readiness and spec quality gates

expected_evidence.description: Shape-spec scenarios prove acceptance criteria, dependency declaration, prioritization readiness, clarifying questions, edge-case detection, oversized split proposals, untestable readiness blocks, and not-ready explanations

nodes.scope: Implement the shaped spec model, acceptance criteria, dependency declarations, readiness checks, spec quality findings, split proposals, and readiness explanations.
nodes.rationale: Current near-ready shaping primitives are useful, but the rewrite should anchor them in accepted roadmap and proof obligations.
risks: Quality checks can become advisory text instead of lifecycle guards
shape_notes: Promote the already-implemented candidates only after behavior proof is added.

nodes.title: Implement spec lifecycle, grouping, and dependency state

expected_evidence.description: Spec lifecycle scenarios prove ready/backlog/archive transitions, state visibility, bulk actions, milestone and initiative grouping, and cross-project dependency blocking

nodes.scope: Create event-backed spec states, backlog movement, ready marking, archiving, bulk actions, milestones, initiatives, and dependency records.
nodes.rationale: Orchestration needs typed spec lifecycle state rather than temporary command markdown.
risks: Grouping can drift into portfolio management before execution needs are met
shape_notes: Keep lifecycle rules focused on execution eligibility and visibility.

nodes.title: Implement loop start, state, pause, resume, cancellation, and eligibility

expected_evidence.description: Loop scenarios prove manual start, serial auto-start, state visibility, pause/resume, dependency blocking, and cancellation through shared orchestration state

nodes.scope: Implement active spec loop state, start eligibility, serial auto-start, dependency blocking, pause/resume, and cancellation transitions.
nodes.rationale: Existing loop behaviors are close but divergent because they do not yet run through the shared API, event, and dependency model.
risks: A compatibility shim around old loop code could preserve the wrong state model
shape_notes: Rewrite the state transitions first; salvage existing command semantics only where they match the architecture.

nodes.title: Implement blockers, notifications, and attention routing

expected_evidence.description: Blocker scenarios prove attention routing, responder selection, notifications, and user response while a loop is paused

nodes.scope: Add blocker records, routed questions, attention read models, notification events, response capture, and unblocking transitions.
nodes.rationale: Human judgment remains required at blockers; the loop must surface exact attention needs without losing execution state.
risks: Notification delivery could be implemented before routing semantics are stable
shape_notes: Keep notification preferences as dependency on configuration; use default routing initially.

nodes.title: Implement live activity and team coordination

expected_evidence.description: Team coordination scenarios prove active-work visibility, assist/takeover, ownership transfer, takeover requests, action history, overlap prevention, handoff notes, assignments, onboarding, attention load, and candidate coordination

nodes.scope: Add active loop activity streams, ownership, assignment, assist/takeover rules, overlap detection, handoff notes, action history, and grouped active-work views.
nodes.rationale: Parallel agent and team work needs explicit coordination before runtime parallelism becomes useful.
risks: People metrics can accidentally become productivity scoring
shape_notes: Expose coordination state, not individual performance rankings.

nodes.title: Implement runtime harness configuration and readiness

expected_evidence.description: Harness readiness scenarios prove credential presence, harness selection, capability preflight, and readiness/blocked explanations

nodes.scope: Implement harness credential metadata, project harness selection, harness capability discovery, and readiness read models for Codex, Claude Code, and OpenCode adapter families.
nodes.rationale: Runtime execution should be harness-neutral and policy-aware before actual assignments run.
risks: Credential values can leak if readiness checks inspect secrets directly
shape_notes: Use metadata and scoped use operations only; secret storage arrives through the governance milestone.

nodes.title: Implement isolated execution target placement

expected_evidence.description: Runtime placement scenarios prove isolated target start, active-location visibility, environment access visibility, remote target management, drain/retire, queue/target health, and placement approval audit

nodes.scope: Create target classes, placement decisions, queue/target health, active execution location, execution access summaries, remote target management, retirement/drain, and placement audit records.
nodes.rationale: The accepted architecture rejects unmanaged local worktrees; execution needs isolated targets as a product baseline.
risks: Docker-local implementation can accidentally become a non-portable assumption
shape_notes: Keep target class contracts orchestrator-neutral even if local containers are implemented first.

nodes.title: Implement assignment queue, lease, retry, cancellation, and recovery

expected_evidence.description: Worker-contract scenarios prove scoped assignment receipt/refusal, progress, artifacts, blockers, terminal outcomes, cancellation, idempotent retry, failure classification, and interrupted-session reconciliation

nodes.scope: Implement durable assignments, queue claims, leases, heartbeats, cancellation, retries, terminal outcomes, worker progress, artifacts, blockers, failure taxonomy, and reconciliation.
nodes.rationale: Runtime must report bounded durable outcomes without reinterpreting product semantics.
risks: Retry paths can duplicate visible work if assignment identity is weak
shape_notes: Tie every assignment to spec/task/phase context and idempotency records.

nodes.title: Implement worker-scoped access and redacted runtime output

expected_evidence.description: Access and redaction scenarios prove temporary grants, revocation, granted-access-only execution, and result preservation without leaking secrets

nodes.scope: Add temporary worker access grants, assignment-scoped credentials, revocation, capability checks, output redaction, and bounded result persistence.
nodes.rationale: The readiness assessment calls out this behavior as close; it should be completed in the new runtime model rather than patched into old output paths.
risks: Redaction after persistence is too late
shape_notes: Redact before logs, events, proof records, and interface responses.

nodes.title: Implement executable behavior proof

expected_evidence.description: Proof scenarios prove behavior-to-proof linkage, positive witnesses, falsification witnesses, proof results, staleness, and assertion read models

nodes.scope: Create behavior proof targets, BDD linkage, positive/falsification witness classification, proof runs, assertion read models, mutation-quality inputs, and proof staleness.
nodes.rationale: Behavior proof is the executable bridge between accepted behavior and delivery completion.
risks: Unit tests may be overcounted as behavior proof
shape_notes: Feature files should normally prove exactly one behavior ID.

nodes.title: Implement quality controls, findings, audits, and proactive analysis

expected_evidence.description: Quality-control scenarios prove finding lifecycle, codebase-wide audit, proactive analysis scheduling/runs, planning routing, and recommendation review

nodes.scope: Add finding lifecycle, codebase-wide audit runs, proactive analysis schedules, analysis result review, and routing into planning proposals or spec candidates.
nodes.rationale: Analysis should feed the method rather than bypassing roadmap and spec shaping.
risks: Analysis findings may become noisy if provenance and uncertainty are weak
shape_notes: Normalize source findings and keep human review available for uncertain recommendations.

nodes.title: Implement walk, demo, and acceptance records

expected_evidence.description: Walk scenarios prove demo summary, expected-vs-actual behavior, changed surfaces, residual risks, acceptance records, rejection routing, and trigger from implementation-ready work

nodes.scope: Implement run-demo outputs, walk-spec sessions, behavior review, acceptance/rejection, residual risk capture, acceptance preservation, and behavior-affecting staleness.
nodes.rationale: The product method needs human behavior acceptance as a separate channel from code review.
risks: Run-demo output can be confused with human walk acceptance
shape_notes: Keep autonomous demo critique and interactive walk acceptance as separate events.

nodes.title: Implement PR, CI, review feedback, merge-ready, and cleanup flow

expected_evidence.description: Review and merge scenarios prove draft PR creation, CI visibility, review-feedback routing, addressed/out-of-scope dispositions, merge acceptance, cleanup, merge-conflict recovery, intent-conflict detection, and stacked-diff execution

nodes.scope: Add source-control handoff for draft PRs, CI status consumption, review-feedback routing, merge-ready state, merge handoff, cleanup, merge-conflict recovery, intent-conflict detection, and stacked-diff dependent specs.
nodes.rationale: Delivered work should not count as shipped until proof, walk, review, and merge flow are durable and explainable.
risks: Provider-specific mechanics can leak into orchestration semantics
shape_notes: Keep source-control provider calls behind integration adapters.

nodes.title: Implement release learning and shipped outcomes

expected_evidence.description: Release-learning scenarios prove release notes, post-release verification, shipped links to roadmap/specs, feedback intake, roadmap updates, and configurable shipped definition

nodes.scope: Add shipped-definition configuration, release-note preparation, post-release checks, shipped links to roadmap/specs, feedback intake, and roadmap/spec update routing.
nodes.rationale: The method is cumulative only if shipped outcomes feed back into planning and assessment.
risks: Release learning may be delayed if provider integration is incomplete
shape_notes: Support manual shipped records first, then provider-backed automation.

nodes.title: Implement organization access, roles, memberships, and project grants

expected_evidence.description: Governance scenarios prove project access, role templates, permissions visibility, organization policy, permission history, invitations, join/leave/remove, organization deletion disposition, and project/organization access management

nodes.scope: Implement permissions, role templates, grants, membership invitations, member lifecycle, project access, organization access, policy history, and deletion disposition.
nodes.rationale: Governance is core for safe automation, even for solo installs that later become team installs.
risks: Role templates can be mistaken for authorization roles
shape_notes: Authorization checks permission grants, not personas or role names.

nodes.title: Implement approvals and autonomy boundaries

expected_evidence.description: Approval scenarios prove autonomy level configuration, sensitive-action approval requirements, low-risk continuation, boundary stops, approval state, approval collection, policy simulation, and denial explanations

nodes.scope: Add autonomy configuration, approval policy, gated action state, approval collection, policy dry-runs, denial explanations, and automation boundary enforcement.
nodes.rationale: Agentic execution must stop at explicit human and policy boundaries.
risks: Approvals can become interface-only prompts without durable policy state
shape_notes: Approval satisfaction and denial explanations should be event-backed and audit-visible.

nodes.title: Implement configuration inheritance and standards policy

expected_evidence.description: Configuration scenarios prove user/project/organization/account settings, cross-device sync, notification preferences, standards policy, policy inheritance/overrides, runtime defaults, verification gates, change history, and shared project defaults

nodes.scope: Add scoped configuration records, effective-setting resolution, inheritance/override/lock semantics, notification preferences, standards policy, runtime defaults, verification gates, and configuration history.
nodes.rationale: The system needs typed configuration before runtime, standards, and verification behavior can be safely customized.
risks: A single global precedence ladder will not fit all setting families
shape_notes: Each setting family declares its own resolution rules.

nodes.title: Implement secrets, credentials, service accounts, and API keys

expected_evidence.description: Credential scenarios prove non-exposure, shared secret storage, secret usage visibility, rotation, revocation, personal/shared distinction, service accounts, API keys, overscoped/stale detection, credential-use policy, and API key rotation/revocation

nodes.scope: Implement write-only/use-only secret storage, credential metadata, secret usage records, personal/shared ownership, service accounts, API keys, rotation, revocation, stale/overscoped detection, and credential-use policy.
nodes.rationale: Execution and integrations require credentials, but secret values must never become events, projections, logs, proof, or interface responses.
risks: Secret-store shortcuts can compromise the event-sourced model
shape_notes: Store secret metadata canonically; store values behind the secret-store abstraction.

nodes.title: Implement runtime placement, harness, and budget policy

expected_evidence.description: Policy scenarios prove runtime placement policy, harness allowlist policy, and budget/quota policy affecting execution eligibility and placement

nodes.scope: Connect runtime placement, harness allowlists, budget/quota limits, and approval requirements to execution placement and readiness decisions.
nodes.rationale: Parallelism, remote execution, and provider use must remain policy-controlled optimizations.
risks: Policy can become hard-coded runtime conditionals
shape_notes: Policy evaluation should produce explainable decisions consumed by runtime.

nodes.title: Implement provider integration management

expected_evidence.description: Provider scenarios prove organization/user connections, personal/shared access distinction, connection health, permissions and reachable resources, authorization recovery, and external action audit

nodes.scope: Implement provider connection records, auth state, health, permission/resource discovery, recovery flows, provider action audit, and personal versus shared provider access.
nodes.rationale: Provider adapters should be replaceable and governed by Tanren policy instead of embedded in workflow code.
risks: Provider-specific payloads can leak into core contracts
shape_notes: Normalize adapter state and retain raw payloads only where policy permits.

nodes.title: Implement external tracker and outbound issue integration

expected_evidence.description: External tracker scenarios prove connection, unshaped ticket visibility, outbound issue state, review mode, external reference state, quick links, disconnect/replace, and queued outbound issue review

nodes.scope: Add external tracker connection, unshaped ticket ingestion, outbound issue queue/review, external reference state, quick links, and disconnect/replace flow.
nodes.rationale: Tanren remains the system of record for specs while supporting one-way outbound tracker integration.
risks: Tracker integration can pull Tanren toward ticket-tracker semantics
shape_notes: Keep specs canonical in Tanren and treat trackers as external references.

nodes.title: Implement webhooks, external client attribution, CI status, and provider backpressure

expected_evidence.description: Client integration scenarios prove webhook retry/ordering/dedupe, external automation attribution, permission-boundary denial, CI/source-control status reporting, webhook endpoint management, and delivery failure handling

nodes.scope: Implement webhook endpoints, delivery outbox, retry/dedupe/ordering, external automation actor attribution, machine-client permission boundaries, external CI/status reporting, and delivery failure management.
nodes.rationale: External automation must be auditable and idempotent rather than a side channel around Tanren state.
risks: Webhook delivery can create duplicate side effects without durable outbox keys
shape_notes: Use event/outbox records as the source for delivery and replay.

nodes.title: Implement project, roadmap, blocked-work, and provenance observation

expected_evidence.description: Observation scenarios prove project attention, scoped views, roadmap progress, blocked overview, source-signal provenance, open decisions, mission or standards drift, and cross-project dependency risk

nodes.scope: Build observation read models for project lists, scoped views, roadmap progress, blocked work, source-signal provenance, open decisions, mission or standards drift, and cross-project dependency risk.
nodes.rationale: This gives Tanren-in-Tanren useful planning and blocked-work visibility before delivery metrics are mature.
risks: Missing data can be incorrectly reported as healthy or zero
shape_notes: Every observation claim should expose freshness, completeness, bounds, and provenance.

nodes.title: Implement pipeline, quality, health, and delivery observation

expected_evidence.description: Observation scenarios prove work pipeline, quality signals, health signals, time-window comparison, contextual per-builder breakdown, delivery forecast and risk, quality risk trends, and planned-versus-actual delivery

nodes.scope: Build observation read models for work pipeline, quality and health signals, time-window comparison, contextual per-builder breakdown, forecasts, quality risk trends, and planned-versus-actual delivery.
nodes.rationale: Delivery metrics require orchestration, runtime, quality, review, and shipped-outcome signals, so they should not block earlier planning observation.
risks: Per-builder views can drift into individual productivity scoring
shape_notes: Expose concrete coordination and improvement signals, not leaderboards.

nodes.title: Implement shipped-outcome, report, digest, and changed-since observation

expected_evidence.description: Reporting scenarios prove recently shipped outcomes, post-release health and feedback, read-only status export, observer digest subscription, and changed-since-last-report baselines

nodes.scope: Add durable report snapshots, observer digests, shipped outcome summaries, post-release health/feedback summaries, exportable read-only reports, and changed-since-last-report baselines.
nodes.rationale: Reports and digests are durable observation artifacts, not screenshots of live dashboards.
risks: Reports can leak redacted state through aggregation
shape_notes: Apply redaction before summarization and persist report baselines.

nodes.title: Implement operations, backup, restore, pause, incident, quota, and audit export

expected_evidence.description: Operations scenarios prove export, restore, pause/resume new work, cost/quota tracking, incident/maintenance mode, disaster recovery validation, and operational audit log export

nodes.scope: Add data export/restore, scoped pause/resume, cost/quota tracking, incident and maintenance mode, disaster recovery validation, and operational audit export.
nodes.rationale: Self-hosted operators need recoverability and operational control before broad team use is credible.
risks: Restore behavior can conflict with append-only event semantics if modeled as overwrite
shape_notes: Represent restore and recovery as controlled operations with audit-visible events.

nodes.title: Implement repository asset bootstrap, drift, generated integrations, and standards root

expected_evidence.description: Repository asset scenarios prove bootstrap, drift detection, selected agent integration generation, standards root loading, standards discovery/update proposals, standards influence explanations, conflict detection, standards profile management, and profile application

nodes.scope: Implement generated repository assets, installer drift detection, selected harness/agent integrations, runtime standards root loading, standards source discovery, standards update proposals, influence explanations, conflict detection, organization standards profiles, and profile application.
nodes.rationale: Repo-local artifacts remain valuable, but they should be projections or controlled install artifacts rather than source of truth.
risks: Installer code can reintroduce direct file mutation as canon
shape_notes: Generated assets should include ownership metadata and drift fingerprints.

nodes.title: Implement self-hosted stack packaging, upgrade, and uninstall

expected_evidence.description: Delivery scenarios prove upgrade of installed Tanren assets and uninstall without deleting user work

nodes.scope: Package upgrade bundles, migration invocation, asset upgrade, stack/repo uninstall separation, and uninstall safety checks for user-owned work.
nodes.rationale: Delivery has to preserve the same architecture posture through install, upgrade, and removal.
risks: Uninstall can accidentally delete user-owned project work
shape_notes: Keep stack uninstall and repository asset uninstall separate.
